local_help.--help:
usage: goofile [-h] [-d DOMAIN] [-f FILETYPE] [-k KEY] [-e ENGINE] [-q QUERY]
               [--logging LOGGING]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -d, --domain DOMAIN   the domain to search - optional (ie. kali.org
  -f, --filetype FILETYPE
                        the filetype to search for - required (ie. pdf)
  -k, --key KEY         Google Custom Search Engine API key - optional
  -e, --engine ENGINE   Google Custom Search Engine ID - optional
  -q, --query QUERY     Only search for files with keyword - optional
  --logging LOGGING     Set the logging verbosity to something other than
                        "INFO" - optional
local_help.-h:
usage: goofile [-h] [-d DOMAIN] [-f FILETYPE] [-k KEY] [-e ENGINE] [-q QUERY]
               [--logging LOGGING]

  -k, --key KEY         Google Custom Search Engine API key - optional
  -e, --engine ENGINE   Google Custom Search Engine ID - optional
  -q, --query QUERY     Only search for files with keyword - optional
  --logging LOGGING     Set the logging verbosity to something other than
                        "INFO" - optional

usage: goofile [-h] [-d DOMAIN] [-f FILETYPE] [-k KEY] [-e ENGINE] [-q QUERY]

apt_metadata.description:
Command line filetype search
Use this tool to search for a specific file type in a given domain.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use goofile because ### goofile.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `goofile [-h] [-d DOMAIN] [-f FILETYPE] [-k KEY] [-e ENGINE] [-q QUERY]` as documented upstream for goofile.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair goofile with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `goofile --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: goofile [-h] [-d DOMAIN] [-f FILETYPE] [-k KEY] [-e ENGINE] [-q QUERY]
